response:
## src/billing.ts — what it does

Inferred from the scan: a 310-line billing module with 5 top-level functions. The two named ones are:

- **`generateInvoice` (L50–253)** — 204-line invoice generator. Reads the system clock 7 times (lines 57, 63, 92, 198, 238, 250, 273) for timestamps/dates.
- **`calculateRefundPreview` (L40–44)** — name suggests pure calculation but actually calls `saveRefundAudit` and `sendRefundEmail` (side effects). A nearby comment at L39 says "Only owners can refund enterprise plans unless billing support approves" — a rule that isn't visibly encoded in the code.

The file also references the `"enterprise"` plan literal (L7) shared with three other files.

## Related files to read before editing

- **`src/api/plan.ts`, `src/jobs/plan-sync.ts`, `src/ui/pricing.ts`** — all four files duplicate the `"enterprise"` string literal; any plan-policy edit here likely needs to land there too.
- **`src/billing.test.ts`** — the test suite for this file is weak: `"renders the enterprise plan"` (L4–6) has no asserts at all, and `"creates an invoice"` (L8–10) uses only weak matchers. Don't trust passing tests as protection.
- Wherever **owner / billing-support role checks** live (not surfaced by the scan) — the L39 comment implies an authorization rule that may or may not exist in code; worth grepping for before changing refund logic.

## Risks the scan flagged on this file

| # | Severity | Charge | Where |
|---|---|---|---|
| 00002 | high | God Function — `generateInvoice` is 3.4× the 60-line threshold | L50–253 |
| 00009 | medium | Temporal Recklessness — 7 direct `Date.now()` / `new Date()` calls | L57–273 |
| 00012 | medium | String Sprinkles — `"enterprise"` literal duplicated across 4 files | L2 (this file at L7) |
| 00013 | medium | False Identity — `calculateRefundPreview` does side effects | L40–44 |
| 00016 | medium | God File — 310 lines, just over the 300 threshold | whole file |
| 00017 | medium | Logic in the Alibi — owner/refund rule lives in a comment | L39 |
| 00035 | low | Unfinished Business — 4 TODO/FIXME markers | L183–308 |

The combination that most warrants caution before editing: **the L39 comment rule + the misleading `calculateRefundPreview` name + the weak/missing tests**. An agent editing refund logic here has neither type-level nor test-level guardrails for the owner/billing-support invariant.
